claim: Reasoning models paired with tools and an environment, plus training on specific tasks, constitute an agent
actionable: When building agentic systems, combine a reasoning model with tools, environment access, guardrails, and task-specific training rather than treating the model alone as sufficient

claim: Sandboxing increases (not decreases) with auto mode because the agent runs in its own computer with limited file system access
actionable: When enabling autonomous agent capabilities, deploy agents in isolated computing environments to maintain safety even as autonomy increases

claim: PR descriptions should be concise, highlight relevant code sections, and cite specific lines so humans can efficiently review AI-generated changes
actionable: When prompting or training coding agents, require them to generate short PR titles and descriptions that cite relevant code snippets for human reviewers

claim: Agents should attempt to test their code changes and report test results with deterministic references to logs for human verification
actionable: Implement automated testing verification in agent workflows where agents run tests and cite log references so humans can independently verify results

claim: Agents should infer code style from existing codebases rather than requiring explicit instructions, reducing token usage and improving consistency
actionable: Train coding agents to analyze existing code style in a repository and apply the same patterns automatically without explicit style instructions

claim: agents.md files let agents discover instructions hierarchically by subdirectory, acting as time-efficient exploration compression vs full test-time discovery
actionable: Create agents.md files in subdirectories with role-specific instructions to guide agent exploration and reduce token spent on discovering patterns

claim: Linters and formatters are essential agent-loop verifiers that improve both AI output quality and human code hygiene
actionable: Require linters and auto-formatters in agent-using codebases so agents can use them to self-verify changes before submitting

claim: Commit hooks are beneficial for agents (unlike many human developers who resent them) because they enable automated verification of changes
actionable: Enable commit hooks in repositories where coding agents operate to provide automated guardrails that agents can leverage

claim: Modular, testable code architecture becomes more critical for agent productivity because agent commit velocity drops significantly in monolithic codebases
actionable: Prioritize modular architecture and clear separation of concerns when teams plan to delegate tasks to coding agents

claim: Treat agents as a long-horizon independent capability (30 min+ thinking time is reasonable) rather than quick helpers, requiring shift from instant-feedback mental models
actionable: When delegating tasks to autonomous coding agents, submit multiple tasks in parallel and check results later rather than watching progress

claim: Codebase naming conventions should be intentional for agent discoverability: unique substrings (like WHAM) allow efficient prompt-less reference
actionable: Choose internal codebase names that don't collide with common substrings to allow agents to locate code without redundant path specifications

claim: Avoid JavaScript; use at minimum TypeScript with type annotations to improve agent code comprehension and reduce errors
actionable: Ensure all codebases where agents will operate use typed languages (TypeScript minimum) rather than untyped JavaScript

claim: Train models on diverse task examples during pre-training rather than relying solely on deterministic prompts or guardrails for better generalization
actionable: Invest in training models with diverse examples of correct behavior rather than adding post-hoc constraint layers when building production agents

claim: Three-part user decision framework for agents: user decides upfront, developer decides better than model, model decides best—each decision should map to exactly one party
actionable: When designing agent UI and product logic, explicitly assign each decision point to user, developer, or model based on which party makes better choices

claim: Specialized models trained on specific domains can transfer learnings back to general models, potentially yielding outsized returns via transfer learning
actionable: Build specialized models for specific use cases (e.g., code generation) with confidence that insights will scale back to general-purpose models
